input.title: Runtime Polymorphism

output.technical: The `virtual` keyword enables dynamic dispatch via a vtable. When a method is called on a base pointer pointing to a derived object, the derived class's overridden method is executed at runtime.
output.beginner: Virtual functions let a parent object magically behave like its child object. Even though the computer thinks it's holding a Base object, it runs the Derived version of the function.
output.analogy: Imagine a TV remote with a 'Play' button (Base). Whether you point it at a DVD player or a PlayStation (Derived), the correct machine figures out how to 'Play'.
output.solution: Always declare base class destructors as `virtual` to ensure proper cleanup of derived objects.
output.prevention: Use the `override` keyword in derived classes so the compiler catches typos in function signatures.

input.title: Runtime Polymorphism

output.technical: The `virtual` keyword enables dynamic dispatch via a vtable. When a method is called on a base pointer pointing to a derived object, the derived class's overridden method is executed at runtime.
output.beginner: Virtual functions let a parent object magically behave like its child object. Even though the computer thinks it's holding a Base object, it runs the Derived version of the function.
output.analogy: Imagine a TV remote with a 'Play' button (Base). Whether you point it at a DVD player or a PlayStation (Derived), the correct machine figures out how to 'Play'.
output.solution: Always declare base class destructors as `virtual` to ensure proper cleanup of derived objects.
output.prevention: Use the `override` keyword in derived classes so the compiler catches typos in function signatures.
